summary: GPS Accuracy Feature Testing PASSED - All 8 backend tests pass (100%). Backend correctly flags submissions with gps_accuracy > 2.0 as gps_low_confidence: true. Frontend GPS UI elements (card, badges, progress bar, refresh button) are properly implemented with correct data-testid attributes. Login and dashboard regression tests pass.
test_credentials: Used GM (ctyler.gm@slmco.local) with password SLMCo2026!, Crew access code: 000a07ca
seed_data_creation: Created 4 test submissions with varying GPS accuracy values (1.5m, 2.0m, 2.1m, 5.5m) to verify gps_low_confidence flag
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 17 verified GPS accuracy improvement feature. Backend POST /api/public/submissions correctly sets gps_low_confidence: true when gps_accuracy > 2.0 and false when gps_accuracy <= 2.0. Frontend CrewCapturePage.jsx has GPS polling with watchPosition (10 second timeout), accuracy indicator badges (Precise/Fair/Low confidence), progress bar animation, and 'Flagged for review' badge for accuracy > 2m. Note: GPS UI elements only render when geolocation is acquired - headless browser testing cannot fully test GPS acquisition flow. CSS animation @keyframes gpsProgress defined in App.css lines 158-161.

frontend_gps_ui_elements.crew-gps-accuracy-badge: Present in code (renders when GPS acquired)
